The Numbers That Matter

Why Pocket Broth
changes the category.

−54%
Lighter than equivalent calorie freeze dried meals

48g total carried weight vs 104g for a BackCountry Cuisine single serve. Over a 7-day trip that’s 392g saved in weight alone.

8×
Less pack volume

We blend, dehydrate, powder and vacseal to create the smallest form factor to save the maximum space in your pack.

30s
From sachet to soup

Add 450ml boiling water, stir, and hey presto! Boiling hot soup. No in-bag rehydration, no 10–15 minute wait, no altitude failures.

Zero
Fillers, enhancers, or shortcuts

No maltodextrin. No MSG. No modified starch. Every recipe starts as real cooked soup — the ingredient list reads like a recipe.

500g
Of thick tasty soup

That’s close to 10× volume, creating a healthy, hearty and filling lunch.

Healthy
Delicious hand crafted recipes

A range of recipes designed to pack the most bang for buck — tasty, filling and using local ingredients.

The Launch Range

Four flavours · Australian illustrated · Kraft paper packaging

The Problem

Trail food hasn’t
kept up with hikers.

The ultralight community obsesses over every gram — tents, poles, cookware. Then reaches for a soup pouch the size of a novel.

Problem 01
Too Heavy

A standard competitor single serve weighs 104g total. Over a week that’s 728g in soup alone — more than most ultralight sleeping bags.

Problem 02
Too Bulky

Stand-up pouches carry dead volume from trailhead to camp. That headspace occupies prime pack real estate every single day.

Problem 03
Too Slow

10 to 15 minutes rehydration. At altitude where boiling point drops. In the rain after a big day. The wait matters.

Problem 04
Full of Fillers

Maltodextrin. Flavour enhancers. Modified starch. Even the “premium” category relies on compound powders.

The size difference
is hard to argue with.

Why It Wins

Six things no
other trail soup does.

01 ·
Weight
48g total. 54% lighter.

Over a 7-day trip, Pocket Broth saves 392g in soup alone — more than most ultralight pole setups.

02 ·
Size
110 × 70 × 25mm.

Smaller than a smartphone. Fits in your top pocket, running vest, or jersey.

03 ·
Packability
Flat. Zero dead volume.

Compresses to nothing when empty. Six sachets stack to 48mm.

04 ·
Rehydration
30 seconds.

Pour 450ml boiling water, stir, done. No 10–15 minute wait in the dark after a hard day.

05 ·
Flavour
Cooked from scratch.

Not assembled from compound powders. No aftertaste, no MSG, no metallic notes.

06 ·
Clean
Zero nasties.

No maltodextrin. No flavour enhancers. No modified starch. FODMAP-conscious options available.

A real gap in
a growing market.

~$1.3B
Global dehydrated hiking food market

Online sales share in the category
Zero
Existing clean-label powdered UL soup brands

The ultralight hiking community is vocal, brand-loyal, and influential. A product that genuinely serves them earns authentic reach. The same sachet then scales into office, ski, overlanding, and emergency markets without reformulation.

One product.
Multiple markets.

The UL hiking community is the beachhead. The same sachet then scales naturally into adjacent spaces.

🎒

The primary beachhead. Obsessive about weight, vocal online, drives category review culture.

💼

🏔️

Calorie density and low volume both critical. Works at altitude where boil times drop.

🚐
Overlanding

Flat storage stacks efficiently in limited space. Clean ingredients over pack weight.
